bibliomixed: Bosak, J. XML, Java, and the future of the Web. World Wide Web Journal 2(4), 1997. https://www.xml.com/pub/a/w3j/s3.bosak.html.
Sperberg-McQueen, C. M. Markup systems and the future of scholarly text processing. Communications of the ACM 34(11):63–76, 1991.
Sperberg-McQueen, C. M. and C. Huitfeldt. GODDAG: A Data Structure for Overlapping Hierarchies. DDEP/PODDP 2000: Principles of Digital Document Processing, pp. 139–160. Springer, Berlin, 2000.

Sperberg-McQueen, C. M., C. Huitfeldt, and A. Renear. Markup and Meaning: What's a Text Really Made Of? (aka "Documents as LMNL"). Delivered at ALLC/ACH 2004, Gothenburg. Revised 2007. http://www.tei-c.org/Vault/Talks/2004/lmnl.html.
Burnard, L. and C. M. Sperberg-McQueen (eds.). TEI P5: Guidelines for Electronic Text Encoding and Interchange. Text Encoding Initiative Consortium, 2007–. https://tei-c.org/guidelines/p5/.

Sperberg-McQueen, C. M. Rabbit/Duck Grammars: A Characterization of Overlap. Proceedings of Balisage: The Markup Conference 2013. Balisage Series on Markup Technologies, vol. 10. doi:10.4242/BalisageVol10.Sperberg-McQueen01.

Chamberlin, D., J. Robie, and D. Florescu. Quilt: An XML Query Language for Heterogeneous Data Sources. Revised Papers from WebDB 2000: Third International Workshop on the Web and Databases, pp. 53–62. Springer, Berlin, 2000.
Robie, J. XML Processing and Data Integration with XQuery. IEEE Internet Computing 11(4):62–67, July–August 2007. doi:10.1109/MIC.2007.96.
Boag, S., D. Chamberlin, M. F. Fernández, D. Florescu, J. Robie, and J. Siméon (eds.). XQuery 1.0: An XML Query Language. W3C Recommendation, 23 January 2007. https://www.w3.org/TR/xquery/.
Fernández, M., A. Malhotra, J. Marsh, M. Nagy, and N. Walsh (eds.). XQuery 1.0 and XPath 2.0 Data Model (XDM). W3C Recommendation, 23 January 2007. https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath-datamodel/.
Katz, H. (ed.). XQuery from the Experts: A Guide to the W3C XML Query Language. Addison-Wesley, Boston, 2004.
Robie, J., M. Dyck, and J. Spiegel (eds.). XML Path Language (XPath) 3.1. W3C Recommendation, 21 March 2017. https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath-31/.
Robie, J., M. Dyck, and J. Spiegel (eds.). XQuery 3.1: An XML Query Language. W3C Recommendation, 21 March 2017. https://www.w3.org/TR/xquery-31/.
Chamberlin, D., J. Robie, and D. Florescu (eds.). XML Query Use Cases. W3C Working Group Note, 23 March 2007. https://www.w3.org/TR/xquery-use-cases/.
Chamberlin, D. and J. Robie (eds.). XQuery 3.1 Use Cases. W3C Working Group Note, 8 December 2015. https://www.w3.org/TR/xquery-31-use-cases/.

Buneman, P., S. Davidson, G. Hillebrand, and D. Suciu. A Query Language and Optimization Techniques for Unstructured Data. Proceedings of the 1996 ACM SIGMOD International Conference on Management of Data, pp. 505–516, 1996.
Abiteboul, S. Querying Semi-Structured Data. Proceedings of the 6th International Conference on Database Theory (ICDT), pp. 1–18. Springer, Berlin, 1997.
Abiteboul, S., D. Quass, J. McHugh, J. Widom, and J. Wiener. The Lorel Query Language for Semistructured Data. International Journal on Digital Libraries 1(1):68–88, 1997.
Goldman, R. and J. Widom. DataGuides: Enabling Query Formulation and Optimization in Semistructured Databases. Proceedings of the 23rd VLDB Conference, pp. 436–445, Athens, Greece, 1997.
Deutsch, A., M. Fernández, D. Florescu, A. Levy, and D. Suciu. A Query Language for XML. Computer Networks 31(11–16):1155–1169, 1999. (Proceedings of the 8th International World Wide Web Conference).

Chamberlin, D., J. Robie, and D. Florescu. XQuery: A Query Language for XML. Proceedings of W3C QL'98 Query Language Workshop, 1998. Updated version presented at WWW9 Conference, 2000. https://www.w3.org/TandS/QL/QL98/pp/xquery.html.
Clark, J. and M. Murata. RELAX NG Tutorial. OASIS Committee Specification, 3 December 2001. https://relaxng.org/tutorial-20011203.html.
Wright, A., H. Andrews, B. Hutton, and G. Dennis (eds.). JSON Schema: A Media Type for Describing JSON Documents. Internet-Draft draft-bhutton-json-schema-01, December 2022. https://json-schema.org/.
Florescu, D. and G. Fourny. JSONiq: The History of a Query Language. IEEE Internet Computing 17(5):86–90, 2013.
Couchbase, Inc. N1QL: A Query Language for JSON. https://www.couchbase.com/products/n1ql. https://docs.couchbase.com/server/current/n1ql/n1ql-language-reference/.
